topics.title: Making a call
topics.faqSummary: Use **`!call`** with a Solana **contract address (CA)** for an official tracked call with caller credit. Wrong CAs add noise; use **`!watch`** if you only want to monitor.
topics.content:
An **official call** is how you put a coin on the serverŌĆÖs radar with **caller credit** tied to you.

Use **`!call`** followed by the tokenŌĆÖs **Solana contract address (CA)**. The bot will fetch live data, post the call, and start tracking performance from that moment.

Tips:
ŌĆó Double-check the CA before you send ŌĆö wrong addresses create noise.
ŌĆó Calls are meant to be intentional; use **`!watch`** if you only want to monitor without taking credit.

topics.title: Watching a coin
topics.faqSummary: **`!watch <CA>`** tracks a token **without** caller creditŌĆögood for monitoring only. Use **`!tracked`** to see whatŌĆÖs on your watchlist.
topics.content:
**`!watch`** adds a token to **tracking** **without** giving you **caller credit**. ItŌĆÖs useful when you want charts, milestones, or context in the feed but arenŌĆÖt making an official call.

Use the same **contract address (CA)** you would use for **`!call`**. To see everything thatŌĆÖs being tracked, use **`!tracked`**.

topics.title: Checking a coin
topics.faqSummary: **`!ca <CA>`** is quick intel without tracking. **`!scan`** / **`!scan <CA>`** are scan-style previewsŌĆöstill no tracking until you **`!call`** or **`!watch`**.
topics.content:
You can **peek at a token** without committing to a call or watch.

ŌĆó **`!ca <address>`** ŌĆö compact **intel** (market context, links, etc.) **without** adding tracking.
ŌĆó **`!scan`** ŌĆö try a **random-style** scan for practice.
ŌĆó **`!scan <address>`** ŌĆö a **deeper scan-style** view for one contract **without** tracking.

Use these when youŌĆÖre researching before you decide to **`!call`** or **`!watch`**.

topics.title: Low-cap watchlist
topics.content:
Many servers use McGBotŌĆÖs **scanner** and **#bot-calls** (or similar channels) to surface **low-cap** and early ideas automatically.

When the scanner is **on**, the bot can run **monitoring** and **auto-call** flows according to your serverŌĆÖs settings. **Mods** control whether the scanner is running (**`!scanner on`** / **`!scanner off`**).

Your **personal watchlist** in the bot is still driven by **`!call`**, **`!watch`**, and **`!tracked`** ŌĆö the scanner is the server-wide layer on top.

topics.title: Dev intelligence
topics.content:
McGBot can tie **tokens** back to **developers** when your server tracks dev wallets and launches.

ŌĆó Use **`!devleaderboard`** to see how tracked devs rank.
ŌĆó **`!addlaunch`** (where your server enables it) can **log a launch** against a dev you already track.

Exact channels and workflows depend on how **your server** set up **dev feed** and **tracked dev** areas ŌĆö ask a mod if youŌĆÖre unsure where to post a wallet or note.

topics.title: Profiles & identity
topics.faqSummary: **`!profile`** / **`!myprofile`** opens your profile. **`!credit`** sets how your name shows on calls (anonymous, Discord name, or verified X tag).
topics.content:
Your **profile** is how McGBot knows **you** across calls and stats.

ŌĆó **`!profile`** or **`!myprofile`** opens your profile (with extra options when itŌĆÖs **your** account).
ŌĆó **`!credit`** lets you choose how your name appears on calls ŌĆö for example **anonymous**, **Discord name**, or a **verified X tag** (once X is linked).

Keeping your profile accurate makes **caller stats** and leaderboards more meaningful.

topics.title: X verification
topics.faqSummary: Link X via **`#verify-x`** and/or **Verify X** on your profile. Mods approve with **`!verifyx`**ŌĆöthat command isnŌĆÖt for regular members.
topics.content:
Linking **X (Twitter)** proves which handle is yours so the bot can show a **consistent public identity** on calls (when you choose that credit mode).

Most servers use a **#verify-x** channel and/or the **Verify X** action on **`!profile`**. Follow the **on-screen steps** your server provides.

**Moderators** approve pending verifications with **`!verifyx`** ŌĆö regular users donŌĆÖt run that command themselves.

topics.title: Membership & premium
topics.content:
**McGBotŌĆÖs core commands** (calls, watch, tracked, scans, profiles) are used inside **your Discord server** under whatever rules **that community** sets.

Some servers add **extra roles**, **private channels**, or **paid memberships** on their own. Those perks are **not** defined by the bot itself ŌĆö theyŌĆÖre up to **your serverŌĆÖs admins**.

If youŌĆÖre unsure what you unlock with a role, ask **@Mods** or read your serverŌĆÖs **announcements**.

topics.title: Trust & reputation
topics.content:
McGBot helps your community **remember calls and context** ŌĆö it does **not** replace **judgment** or **moderation**.

**Trust** builds from **track records** (who called what, how it performed) and from **mods** who curate **bot approvals**, **X verification**, and server rules.

If something looks off, use your serverŌĆÖs **reporting** or **mod** channels. Never treat any bot output as **financial advice**.

topics.title: Caller stats
topics.faqSummary: **`!caller`**, **`!callerboard`**, and **`!botstats`** show performance. Use **`!commands`** for time-window stats your role can access.
topics.content:
**Caller stats** summarize how calls have performed for a person or for the **auto bot**.

ŌĆó **`!caller <name>`** or **`!caller @user`** ŌĆö stats for one caller.
ŌĆó **`!callerboard`** ŌĆö top callers by the botŌĆÖs metrics.
ŌĆó **`!botstats`** ŌĆö aggregate stats for **McGBotŌĆÖs** auto calls.

There are also **time-window** commands (for example **24h / week / month**) depending on what your server enabled ŌĆö type **`!commands`** for the full list your account can see.
